introSection: Corkscrew is a steel roller coaster located at Valleyfair in Shakopee, Minnesota, USA. Built by Arrow Development, Corkscrew opened in 1980 and is nearly identical to its sister roller coaster, Corkscrew, at Cedar Point. The only differences are the addition of a final helix and the omission of the camelback before the vertical loop. Corkscrew is currently the only roller coaster at Valleyfair with inversions.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Orange track with yellow inversions and blue supports. The coaster used to feature blue track and white supports, but was repainted in 2011.

fullText:
Corkscrew is a steel roller coaster located at Valleyfair in Shakopee, Minnesota, USA. Built by Arrow Development, Corkscrew opened in 1980 and is nearly identical to its sister roller coaster, Corkscrew, at Cedar Point. The only differences are the addition of a final helix and the omission of the camelback before the vertical loop. Corkscrew is currently the only roller coaster at Valleyfair with inversions.

Corkscrew opened on May 23, 1980.



Orange track with yellow inversions and blue supports. The coaster used to feature blue track and white supports, but was repainted in 2011.
